briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — steady fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for fall with stained water and stable conditions.

Robertson: Use a Ned rig with a 1/4-ounce mushroom-style jighead and green-pumpkin Ned Ocho on spinning gear (10-pound braid to 6-pound fluorocarbon leader) to target smallmouth holding on boulders in 20 feet of water where baitfish are present.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Robertson: Use a Ned rig with a 1/4-ounce mushroom-style jighead and green-pumpkin Ned Ocho on spinning gear (10-pound braid to 6-pound fluorocarbon leader) to target smallmouth holding on boulders in
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In stained water, use a chartreuse jig head on the traditional Ned rig for added visual edge with a 3-inch stick bait.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: Fall smallmouth fishing is all about location and timing. Cover water fast with reaction baits to find active groups, then slow down with finesse presentations to catch the rest. If fish stop biting a tube, switch to Ned rig or drop shot.
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.
Robertson: In fall, smallmouth stack up on boulder fields in 20 feet of water where baitfish are concentrated. The fish can be found within a very small 60-foot area when they're grouped up.
Yamamoto pro Fukae: Rigging a 3-inch Fat Senko Texas-rigged on a drop-shot with a 3-inch leader replicates a Ned Rig presentation but avoids bottom hang-ups around cover

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable fall conditions create the most predictable fall fishing. Bass follow shad migrations into creek arms and pockets, feeding at 3-15ft. Key on points at creek mouths and secondary flats where bait concentrates.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
